name: health-score-pro
author: longerian
description: Comprehensive health management system integrating 10 best-selling health books' consensus principles. Use when users discuss nutrition, supplements, fitness, diet, anti-aging, anti-inflammation, longevity, or request diet tracking/analysis.

git_config_access.description: Backup scripts use system git configuration for authentication. This may read ~/.gitconfig (user.name, user.email) and use SSH keys from ~/.ssh/ for repository access

external_actions.name: backup
external_actions.description: Backup health data to GitHub repository
external_actions.trigger: Automatic backup after database changes (when user has configured backup) or manual user request
external_actions.auth: User's Git credentials (SSH keys or credential manager)

user_control.disable_option: User can set 'enabled: false' in backup_config.json
user_control.repo_validation: User must provide repository URL during onboarding

security_mitigations: Backup only executes when user has configured backup
User can disable at any time
All scripts are auditable (no obfuscated code)

rationale.exec: Required to run backup scripts for data persistence and git configuration checks
rationale.filesystem: Store user health data in memory/health-users/{username}/ directory
rationale.network: Backup to GitHub repository (user-provided) for data persistence
rationale.scripts: Automated backup system ensures data safety and supports multi-language/timezone features

env.TIMEZONE: User's timezone for proper date/time display in health records
env.LANGUAGE: User's preferred language for all health reports and feedback

rationale.config_paths: Per-user health profiles, daily records, and supplement database for personalized tracking
rationale.git_config_access: Backup functionality requires git credentials for pushing to user's repository

external_actions.backup: Users can backup their health tracking data to GitHub for persistence across sessions

security_notes.data_privacy: User health data stored locally in memory/health-users/{username}/ - only accessible by the user
security_notes.no_exfiltration: Scripts only write to local files and user's own GitHub repository (user provides repo URL)
security_notes.api_keys: No external API keys required. web_search and web_fetch are OpenClaw built-in tools managed by the platform. Git credentials managed by user's system (SSH/credential manager) for optional backup feature
security_notes.scripts_review: All scripts are readable and auditable - no obfuscated code
security_notes.github_backup: Backup feature requires user to provide their own GitHub repository (recommended: private repo). All data transmission is user-controlled
security_notes.network_access: Network access is for: (1) GitHub backup (user-controlled, optional), (2) web_search/web_fetch built-in tools (platform-managed, supports Brave by default, optionally Tavily if configured)
security_notes.audit_recommendation: Users should review all scripts in scripts/ directory before enabling backup. Test with non-sensitive data first. Use private repository for backup.

user_safety_checklist: Review scripts/ directory before enabling backup
Use private GitHub repository for backup
Understand data is stored locally by default
